core_mechanism: a critical-threshold transition between two phases governed by a self-similar (scale-invariant) critical solution at the threshold, with power-law scaling of observables as the control parameter approaches the critical value
brief_reason: The abstract studies a critical solution at the threshold separating two phases and stresses criticality, scaling, and self-similarity analogous to Choptuik critical collapse, which is the signature of a scale-invariant critical-point transition with universal scaling exponents.
